title: The long-term advantage of TSMC
transcript_html:
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/G7UkVXWSDkk

Transcript

Why is TSMC so durable? >> I mean, it's interesting. They don't charge a lot as well. You'd think that if they're a monopoly provider, they should charge a lot of money. They don't. An American capitalist might say, well, they're just they're just screwing up. They they could have extracted more money from the market. But you could also say that um this is actually their long-term sustaining advantage because they will just stay ahead for a really long time. >> They don't encourage the creation of competitors. Isn't the creation of competitors kind of priced in because of geopolitical risk? And so, it's not like everyone's fat, dumb, and happy with their TSMC dependence. They're actually thinking a lot about it. >> Yeah. I mean so there is real technical advantage there as well.
